description: Install dependencies (including Supabase packages), set up Supabase client utilities, create .env.example, create SQL migration file, and configure Next.js middleware for auth session refresh and route protection.
steps: Create .env.example at project root with NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY placeholder values
Create .env.local with the same keys set to placeholder values so build does not fail (values: http://localhost:54321 and placeholder-anon-key)
Create lib/supabase/server.ts - server-side Supabase client using @supabase/ssr with cookie handling for Next.js App Router (uses cookies() from next/headers)
Create lib/supabase/client.ts - browser-side Supabase client using @supabase/ssr createBrowserClient
Create middleware.ts at project root - refreshes auth session on every request, redirects unauthenticated users to /login (except for /login and /signup routes)
Create supabase/migrations/001_create_tasks_table.sql with the tasks table DDL, RLS enablement, and RLS policy as specified in the PRD
Verify build passes: npm run build
acceptance_criteria: middleware.ts exists and exports a config with matcher for protected routes
findings: Next.js 16 deprecates the 'middleware' file convention in favor of 'proxy', but middleware.ts still compiles and works. Build shows warning: 'The middleware file convention is deprecated. Please use proxy instead.' cookies() from next/headers is async in Next.js 16 - must be awaited. @supabase/ssr setAll callback has a second 'headers' parameter for cache-control headers but it is optional. .env* is gitignored by default so .env.example needed git add -f.
